primary_diagnosis.name: Suspected malignant melanoma (evolving, ulcerated/bleeding pigmented lesion)

pathophysiology_summary: A pigmented lesion showing all ABCDE warning features — Asymmetry, Border irregularity (notched/scalloped), Colour variegation (black/brown/grey with peripheral erythema), Diameter >6 mm, and Evolution (rapid change in size/shape/elevation over weeks) — plus the 'ugly-duckling' sign, new pruritus and spontaneous bleeding/ulceration in a person with childhood sunburns, is highly suspicious for malignant melanoma. Melanoma cannot be diagnosed or excluded clinically/remotely; the definitive step is complete EXCISIONAL biopsy with a narrow margin for histopathology and Breslow thickness — partial/shave biopsy of a suspected melanoma is discouraged as it can compromise staging. Spontaneous bleeding here reflects a fragile, ulcerated tumour surface (a mechanical symptom to control with pressure), not the priority; the priority is an urgent fast-track surgical/dermatology referral. Discriminators: a traumatised seborrhoeic keratosis has a 'stuck-on' waxy surface without true colour variegation/irregularity; pigmented BCC grows slowly with pearly rolled borders/arborising vessels; a dysplastic naevus lacks this rapid evolution and spontaneous bleeding. The AI Doctor must avoid false reassurance and expedite excision.

differential.evidence_basis: ABCDE features + rapid evolution + ugly-duckling + bleeding + sun-damage history.
Can bleed if knocked, but lacks true colour variegation/irregular evolution; still needs review to be sure.
Slow-growing with pearly rolled borders — the rapid change here is against it.
Rapid evolution and spontaneous bleeding favour malignancy over a stable dysplastic naevus.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Recognise high melanoma suspicion, avoid false reassurance, and arrange urgent EXCISIONAL biopsy — not a shave/partial biopsy or watchful waiting.

differential.diagnosis: Malignant melanoma (suspected) — for urgent excisional biopsy
differential.evidence_basis: All ABCDE features present; cannot be excluded remotely; histology required.
A benign possibility, but must not be assumed — excision/histology decides.
Working assessment: urgent fast-track excisional biopsy, haemostasis, and psychological support; diagnosis confirmed only on histology.

red_flags.description: Rapidly evolving pigmented lesion (change in size/shape/colour/elevation over weeks)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Evolution is the strongest single melanoma warning sign — mandates urgent excision.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed diagnosis of an invasive melanoma increases Breslow thickness and worsens prognosis.

red_flags.clinical_significance: Multiple ABCDE features with an outlier lesion strongly predict melanoma.

red_flags.description: Spontaneous bleeding/ulceration of a pigmented lesion
red_flags.clinical_significance: Ulceration/bleeding is an adverse prognostic feature; also a mechanical bleeding issue to control.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Telling the patient it's 'probably just a knocked mole' and not arranging urgent excision.
Recommending a shave/partial biopsy of a suspected melanoma, which can compromise Breslow staging.
Managing only the bleeding and not expediting the definitive surgical pathway.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Palpation of the lesion and regional lymph nodes
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Provide haemostasis advice (firm pressure, protective dressing) and arrange an urgent fast-track (within ~1-2 weeks) referral for complete excisional biopsy; ED only if bleeding is uncontrolled. Give honest information and psychological support.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Falsely reassuring the patient or delaying excision worsens melanoma prognosis; a shave biopsy compromises staging.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Suspicious pigmented lesions should undergo complete excisional biopsy with a 2 mm margin for histology and Breslow thickness; avoid partial/shave biopsy of suspected melanoma; refer urgently.
